message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: My feature (remove temperature modulation, add deterministic cache salt) only touches dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py. Please confirm your files are disjoint from this.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: My task: add tip_weights param to GroundedProposer.__init__ and weighted tip selection (_select_tip_with_weights) in grounded_proposer.py. This is the only file my task touches. What does your task touch? If also this file, let's split by region/method so we don't clobber each other - I'll only touch __init__ signature/tip_weights assignment and the set_tip_randomly block in propose_instructions_for_program plus add a new helper method.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: We share this file - let's split by region instead of by file. I will ONLY touch: (1) GroundedProposer.__init__ signature - add a new trailing kwarg 'tip_weights: dict[str, float] | None = None' param and one new 'self.tip_weights = tip_weights' assignment line, added after your existing assignments, not touching your lines; (2) the 'if self.set_tip_randomly:' block inside propose_instructions_for_program - replacing the 'selected_tip_key = self.rng.choice(...)' line with a call to a new helper; (3) adding one new method '_select_tip_with_weights(self)' near the other methods. I will NOT touch rng/temperature/cache-salt logic or any other method. Please confirm this leaves your temperature-modulation/cache-salt changes untouched, or tell me which exact lines you need in __init__ or elsewhere so I can work around them.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Agreed - both touch same file but disjoint regions. Mine: (1) GenerateModuleInstruction.propose_instruction_for_predictor - remove temperature/epsilon modulation, compute cache_salt, pass to instruction_generator call; (2) GenerateModuleInstruction.forward - thread cache_salt param through to describe_program/describe_module/generate_module_instruction calls; (3) generate_instruction_class/DescribeProgram/DescribeModule signature classes - add new cache_salt InputField at the end of each; (4) new private method _cache_salt(self, pred_i, demo_set_i) added to GroundedProposer class. I will NOT touch __init__, tip_weights, set_tip_randomly, or propose_instructions_for_program's tip-selection logic - that's all yours. Will add my new method separately from wherever you add _select_tip_with_weights to avoid insertion collisions - let me know where you're placing yours.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Confirmed disjoint regions, proceeding. Placement to avoid collision: I will insert _select_tip_with_weights(self) as a new method placed immediately AFTER propose_instructions_for_program ends and BEFORE propose_instruction_for_predictor begins. Please place your _cache_salt method elsewhere (e.g. after propose_instruction_for_predictor, or at the end of the class) so our insertions don't land on the same line. I won't touch DescribeProgram/DescribeModule/generate_instruction_class or forward()/propose_instruction_for_predictor bodies. Starting implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Confirmed disjoint. I do not touch __init__, propose_instructions_for_program's tip-selection block, or add _select_tip_with_weights. My edits are confined to: propose_instruction_for_predictor (temperature removal + cache_salt compute/pass), GenerateModuleInstruction.forward (thread cache_salt param), the three signature classes (add cache_salt InputField), and a new _cache_salt method. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Finished my edits: __init__ tip_weights param + assignment, tip-selection call replaced with self._select_tip_with_weights(), and new _select_tip_with_weights method placed between propose_instructions_for_program and propose_instruction_for_predictor. Tested selection logic standalone (uniform draw, weighted bias, invalid-weight fallback, determinism) - all pass. Not touching your regions.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Implementation complete: removed temperature modulation in propose_instruction_for_predictor, added _cache_salt(pred_i, demo_set_i) method to GroundedProposer, added cache_salt InputField to DescribeProgram/DescribeModule/GenerateSingleModuleInstruction signatures, and threaded cache_salt through GenerateModuleInstruction.forward and its three signature calls. Did not touch __init__, propose_instructions_for_program's tip-selection block, or add any tip-weight helper. Existing tests in tests/propose/test_grounded_proposer.py pass (4/4). Wrote patch.txt via git diff. Ready for you to layer your changes on top.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Moved _cache_salt to the end of the class (after propose_instruction_for_predictor) per your placement request, so it no longer collides with your _select_tip_with_weights insertion point between propose_instructions_for_program and propose_instruction_for_predictor. Re-ran tests, all pass. Final patch written to patch.txt.
